section.title: Selected cases
section.subtitle: Examples of projects where I worked on frontend architecture, dashboard systems, real-time functionality, monorepos, design systems, and legacy frontend migration to a modern stack.

rizzult-dashboard.title: Scalable dashboard platform
rizzult-dashboard.preview: Development of a B2B performance creator platform for brands and agencies — clients include KFC, McDonald's, iHerb, and Yango Comida. From a Vue dashboard core to a Nuxt multi-page website with a BFF layer. The product focused on measurable marketing outcomes (ROAS, clicks, installs, sales), creator campaign automation, and transparent analytics for business teams.

hero.title: Scalable dashboard platform
hero.lead: Product dashboard system for data, roles, and user scenarios in a scalable monorepo.

rizzult-dashboard.context: Rizzult is a B2B SaaS performance influencer marketing platform for brands, agencies, and app/e-commerce teams — with clients including KFC, McDonald's, iHerb, and Yango Comida. The product needed a unified system where businesses could run controllable creator campaigns with pay-for-results economics rather than pure reach: multi-country launch flows, transparent action tracking (clicks, installs, sales), ROAS analytics, and scalable operations without expanding the marketing team.
role: Frontend Lead / Fullstack Developer on the product team — led a team of 3 frontend developers
Designed and built the dashboard frontend from scratch: tables, charts, forms, filters, RBAC, and real-time scenarios
Design of FSD architecture and shared modules in the monorepo
Data-heavy screens and interfaces for internal business processes
End-to-end delivery: frontend, REST API, backend on Node.js/NestJS and PHP/Yii2, PostgreSQL, roles, and access control
Code review, mentoring, and AI-assisted development processes inside the team
tasks: Scale the B2B dashboard for business owners, marketers, and agencies with RBAC
Develop the multi-page Nuxt product website with a BFF layer
Establish FSD architecture and shared layers in Turborepo
Build unified data flows for clicks, installs, sales, and ROAS
Implement real-time monitoring with TanStack Query/Table, Highcharts, and Socket.io
Deliver interfaces for tables, forms, filters, and internal operational workflows
Support GitLab CI/CD and improve release stability
solution: Feature-Sliced Design with clear boundaries and ownership
Turborepo for shared packages, configs, and reusable UI
Nuxt + BFF architecture for consistent data across landing pages and dashboard
TanStack Query/Table + Highcharts for robust data-heavy interfaces
Socket.io for live metric updates and a real-time sprint board integrated with YouTrack API
Node.js/NestJS and PHP/Yii2 + PostgreSQL for backend logic, roles, permissions, and external service integrations
AI-assisted development: legacy analysis, boilerplate modules, tests, documentation, shared-logic refactoring, and architectural guardrails
GSAP for UI animations on public pages plus Web Vitals/SEO optimization
GitLab CI/CD + Vitest for reliable delivery and regression control
delivered: B2B dashboard modules for launching and controlling creator campaigns
Dashboard frontend built from scratch: tables, charts, forms, filters, RBAC, and real-time scenarios
Real-time sprint planning board on Socket.io with YouTrack API task synchronization
Multi-page Nuxt website with BFF integration
KPI widgets and reports for clicks, sales, installs, and ROAS
Unified UI/data patterns in the monorepo with lower duplication
GitLab CI/CD pipeline and Vitest coverage for key flows
impact: 35-40% faster delivery of new sections after FSD and shared frontend patterns
Around 30% less code duplication after moving shared UI, types, and utilities into Turborepo packages
Platform scale support: 100,000+ registered influencers
Business-proof campaign tracking scenarios: 37,000,000+ performance actions for brands
Reliable analytics foundation for campaign operations: 6 TB ClickHouse + 100 TB media storage
Strong channel economics reflected in platform outcomes: around 677% average ROAS
Interfaces helped business teams launch campaigns faster and run them with lower operational overhead

interactive-form-ai-dashboard.title: AI startup dashboard & landing
interactive-form-ai-dashboard.preview: Frontend development for an AI no-code SaaS focused on lead generation: landing, interactive form builder, and dashboard for leads and integrations. The product helps businesses launch branded forms in minutes and improve conversion without engineering involvement.

hero.title: AI startup dashboard & landing
hero.lead: Landing and dashboard for an AI product: user flows, settings, and data-driven interfaces in an NX monorepo.

interactive-form-ai-dashboard.context: Interactive Form is a product that generates interactive AI forms from a short business description or a website link. The platform goal was to turn traffic into qualified leads faster via personalized question flows, conversion analytics, and no-code website integration (embed + Google Tag Manager).
role: Development of landing, form generation flow, dashboard, and internal tools
Backend modules on Node.js/NestJS and frontend integration with API
FSD module design in NX monorepo
Implementation of the user flow from signup to first leads
Forms, validation, integrations, and state management for product scenarios
tasks: Build landing, onboarding, and dashboard in one NX monorepo
Implement AI form generation from prompt or website URL
Set up AI-assisted website parsing: extraction rules, quality checks, prompt improvements, and edge cases
Set up lead and API workflows with TanStack Query/Table
Extend backend API endpoints, business logic, and data processing on Node.js/NestJS
Introduce Zod validation for forms and server responses
Add no-code integrations: Google Analytics, TikTok Pixel, Facebook Pixel, and email
Use Zustand where client-side state requires explicit control
solution: Next.js App Router for landing and dashboard flows
NX monorepo with shared UI, API client, and utility libraries
FSD layers for feature isolation and entity reuse
Node.js/NestJS backend: API endpoints, data processing, and integrations for internal tools
AI-assisted parsing: formalized content extraction rules and validation of parsing results
Zod + react-hook-form for type-safe dynamic forms
TanStack Query for predictable server state handling
Conversion tracking integrations and UI flows optimized for fast activation
delivered: Product landing with clear value proposition
AI form creation flow: business prompt -> ready form -> embed code
Dashboard for lead tracking, form management, and integrations
Internal tools for AI-assisted parsing and structured data extraction from websites
Integration screens for analytics, ad pixels, and email notifications
Shared form patterns with typed validation
Modular architecture for parallel feature development
impact: Reduced time-to-lead: users can go from signup to first leads in minutes
Lower launch friction for non-technical teams via no-code form embedding
Fewer form-related issues through schema-driven validation and type-safe API contracts
Strong foundation to scale AI features and pricing tiers without architectural rewrites
Clear structure for code review and team collaboration in a growing product team

perfluence-legacy-migration.title: Legacy frontend migration to a modern stack
perfluence-legacy-migration.preview: Frontend development for a leading influencer/performance marketing platform in Russia and CIS: products for creators and brands, dashboard systems, and Telegram WebView flows. Participated in incremental migration from legacy frontend (PHP templates, jQuery, SCSS) to React/Next.js/TypeScript without pausing delivery.

hero.title: Legacy frontend migration to a modern stack
hero.lead: Incremental transition from PHP/jQuery/SCSS to React/Next.js and TypeScript while continuously shipping product features.

perfluence-legacy-migration.context: Perfluence is a large influencer marketing platform for creators, Telegram channels, and brands in Russia and CIS with a pay-for-results model — 295k+ creators and 200+ client companies. The challenge was to keep shipping new B2B/B2C functionality while migrating legacy frontend to a modern stack without disrupting releases.
role: Fullstack Developer on the product team
Product interface development for creators and brands
Dashboard and Telegram WebView monetization flow development
Participation in legacy migration strategy and implementation without stopping business operations
Backend on PHP/Yii2: API, business logic, PostgreSQL, and support for existing product solutions
REST API integration, analytics, and data visualization
tasks: Ship new React/Next.js features alongside legacy code
Migrate critical jQuery/PHP screens to TypeScript
Develop dashboard flows for funnel, payout, and campaign analytics
Support Telegram WebView and creator monetization scenarios
Account for ad-labeling and legal transparency requirements
Support GitLab CI/CD and test critical flows
solution: Strangler pattern: new React modules, legacy isolated incrementally
AI-assisted migration: legacy decomposition, reusable components, and faster delivery of typical UI flows
Next.js for consistent routing and required SSR/SSG scenarios
Bootstrap UI + SCSS for legacy interface maintenance
Mantine UI for modern modules and faster delivery
PHP/Yii2 + PostgreSQL + REST API for client-server logic and integrations
REST API layer and Highcharts for campaign and payout analytics
Docker + GitLab CI/CD + Jest for stable builds and quality control
delivered: Dashboard screens for creators, brands, and internal teams
Telegram WebView interfaces for monetization scenarios
Migrated key legacy pages without a big-bang rewrite
Charts and reports for campaign efficiency and payouts
Interfaces for offer showcases and promo mechanics
GitLab CI/CD pipeline and Jest coverage for critical modules
impact: Continuous feature delivery during migration without stopping current business operations
Around 50% faster development of new sections after extracting reusable components and API wrappers
25–30% faster delivery of new internal screens through shared table, filter, form, and layout components
40%+ less legacy code in critical user scenarios after incremental migration to React/Next.js
Product support at large audience and campaign scale
Reduced technical debt in critical user flows
More transparent and controllable analytics for the pay-for-results model
Improved maintainability and faster developer onboarding
Single modern stack for new product modules
